steps.title: Identify the contrast between waste categories
steps.content: The passage sets up post-consumer waste (became waste after reaching the consumer) and then immediately pivots to a different category: pre-consumer waste (became waste before reaching the consumer). The defining signal is the opposition between these two temporal categories—one defined by 'after,' the other by 'before.'

steps.title: Apply the contrast to find the transition
steps.content:
'By contrast' marks exactly this opposition between two distinct categories. The alternatives either suggest additional examples of the same concept (For example, Specifically) or imply a causal relationship (As a result), neither of which describes opposing waste types.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
